authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 91 - Consolidated Submissions for Community Planning and Development Programs
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for CPD consolidated planning and annual performance reporting, making it authoritative for CAPER, reporting audience, and public performance-report constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CPD report scope and submission-context qualification
used_for: CAPER and Annual Action Plan reporting posture
public reporting cadence and audience constraints
refusal triggers when CPD submission context is missing

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 570 - Community Development Block Grants
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary CDBG program rule and is authoritative for activity eligibility, national objective support, records, and reporting evidence that govern CDBG-backed development claims.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 92 - HOME Investment Partnerships Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary HOME rule and is authoritative for project setup, completion, commitment, expenditure, affordability, records, and reporting conditions that materially affect housing development metrics.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 58 - Environmental Review Procedures for Entities Assuming HUD Environmental Responsibilities
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is authoritative for environmental review and release-of-funds boundaries that can block readiness, commitment, project-start, and expenditure reporting claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Environmental release and readiness blocker review

authoritative_sources.title: Community Planning and Development Monitoring Handbook 6509.2
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD CPD monitoring guidance is authoritative public operating guidance for records, monitoring evidence, findings, corrective actions, and source-support expectations in CPD-funded programs.
used_for: failure modes for missing official records

authoritative_sources.title: Integrated Disbursement and Information System (IDIS) Online
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD IDIS guidance is authoritative for the source system used by CPD formula grantees to set up activities, fund activities, draw funds, record accomplishments, and produce reports.
used_for: activity, draw, and accomplishment reporting fields

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is authoritative for federal award financial management, internal controls, records, subrecipient monitoring, and audit evidence relevant to CPD-funded reporting packages.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Federal award control and financial-evidence review
used_for: refusal triggers for unsupported expenditure or subrecipient claims

authoritative_sources.title: Guide for Completing Form 8823, Low-Income Housing Credit Agencies Report of Noncompliance or Building Disposition
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The IRS LIHTC guide is authoritative public guidance for compliance-monitoring signals and noncompliance reporting categories that can affect development reporting, while certification stays with compliance or tax authorities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Affordability and LIHTC compliance-signal boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Professional Real Estate Development: The ULI Guide to the Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ULI is a recognized real estate and land-use professional authority. Its development workflow reference supports phase terminology, development milestone artifacts, and boundary conditions for real estate development reports.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Development phase and milestone terminology normalization
used_for: site-control, entitlement, financing, construction, completion, and stabilization terminology
artifact mapping for non-HUD development reporting

authoritative_sources.title: GRESB Real Estate Standard and Reference Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GRESB is a recognized real estate benchmark and disclosure framework. Its real estate assessment materials are authoritative for benchmark evidence, asset and development data validation, and ESG disclosure caveats.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Benchmark or ESG reporting source support review

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and qualification
workflow_stages.description: Collect IDIS, CAPER, HOME, CDBG, environmental review, development pipeline, funding, draw, entitlement, permit, construction, lease-up, affordability, and benchmark records and determine whether the evidence set is current and sufficient for reporting.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and source hierarchy
workflow_stages.description: Resolve unit, completion, commitment, expenditure, beneficiary, affordability, schedule, draw, environmental, and benchmark metric logic before analytics or narrative claims are assembled.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Record normalization and calculation support
workflow_stages.description: Normalize activity-level records across IDIS, CAPER worksheets, project trackers, general ledger, draw systems, document repositories, construction systems, permitting portals, property management systems, and benchmark templates.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis and disclosure-risk review
workflow_stages.description: Assemble report language while checking that no unsupported compliance, causal, community-impact, affordability, environmental, funding, approval, or benchmark claims are introduced.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Submission or reporting package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Package metric definitions, source ties, open blockers, caveats, and evidence dependencies for CAPER, funder, lender, investor, board, internal, or benchmark reporting cadences without implying final authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate stale, conflicting, incomplete, program-sensitive, environmental-sensitive, affordability-sensitive, financing-sensitive, or approval-sensitive cases instead of synthesizing unsupported certainty.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Consolidated Plan, Annual Action Plan, CAPER draft sections, IDIS PR reports, activity setup records, accomplishment records, draw records, and beneficiary or national-objective support
HOME written agreements, project setup and completion records, commitment and expenditure support, affordability-period records, CHDO or subrecipient documents, and project completion evidence
CDBG activity files, matrix code and national-objective support, procurement or subrecipient records, source documentation, and public-service or housing accomplishment support
environmental review record, HEROS status, request for release of funds, authority-to-use-grant-funds status, and responsible-entity approvals

development pipeline tracker, site control, acquisition and disposition records, zoning or entitlement status, permit status, inspection records, certificate of occupancy or temporary certificate records, punch-list posture, construction draw packages, and lease-up or occupancy data

funding commitments, loan or grant agreements, budgets, sources-and-uses, draw ledger, general ledger extracts, capital stack summaries, lender or investor report templates, and board report requirements

LIHTC placed-in-service signals, Form 8609 or Form 8823 status, state housing finance agency compliance portal records, tenant income certification summaries, rent restriction summaries, and compliance-owner caveats
GRESB or benchmark evidence files, asset attributes, performance data, data validation notes, and disclosure caveats where applicable
systems_of_record: eCon Planning Suite or CAPER workspace
HUD DRGR when CDBG-DR or disaster recovery funds are in scope
HEROS or tenant environmental review repository
tenant development pipeline or project management system
construction management or draw management system
accounting, ERP, or general ledger system
loan, grant, funder, lender, or investor reporting portal
state housing finance agency or affordability compliance portal
permitting, entitlement, inspection, and certificate-of-occupancy portal
BI warehouse or reporting data mart

allowed: Classify development reporting requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Define or validate reporting metrics, source lineage, and caveat language from housing development, CPD, funding, project, construction, lease-up, and benchmark records.

Prepare reporting packages, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk escalations without overstating HUD, funder, lender, investor, legal, compliance, environmental, tax, or project approval authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, record locations, metric definitions, program context, jurisdiction, reporting audience, or source precedence rules are missing and materially change the output.
IDIS, CAPER, project tracker, draw ledger, permit, lease-up, HFA, lender, investor, or benchmark records conflict on reportable metrics.

Environmental review status, authority-to-use-grant-funds status, affordability compliance, LIHTC posture, fair housing, procurement, subrecipient, or federal award control issues materially change the reportable answer.

Requests cross into affordable housing compliance, housing finance, real estate development execution, construction draw approval, environmental review, legal, tax, appraisal, lender, investor, or board authority.
refuse: Requests to certify HOME, CDBG, LIHTC, fair housing, environmental review, tax, appraisal, legal, funder, lender, investor, or final project approval conclusions.
Requests to publish unsupported causal, policy, compliance, community-impact, affordability, financial-performance, or final-submission claims not supported by the evidence set.

Requests to approve draws, budgets, change orders, loans, grants, acquisitions, site control, disposition, entitlements, permits, environmental clearance, tenant eligibility, or compliance corrections.

domain_failure_modes: Publishing unit delivery metrics even though certificate-of-occupancy, placed-in-service, HOME completion, lease-up, and stabilized occupancy definitions conflict.
Treating IDIS, CAPER, draw ledger, project tracker, and lender report discrepancies as immaterial and overstating committed funds, beneficiaries, or completed units.
Reporting readiness or project start despite unresolved environmental review or authority-to-use-grant-funds prerequisites.
Using generic real estate pipeline fields without named housing program, funding source, affordability, entitlement, or reporting audience context.
Turning development reporting into LIHTC compliance, fair housing, legal, tax, environmental review, finance approval, construction draw approval, or project execution authority.
Publishing causal community-impact claims, displacement-prevention claims, or benchmark disclosures without evidence that supports the claim and cadence.
Omitting tenant-specific source precedence, funder definitions, lender covenants, or HFA reporting instructions that materially alter the reportable metric.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Submission inputs are incomplete or conflicting
A requested claim is not fully supported by the evidence set

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant source-of-truth for unit delivery, completion, affordability, and commitment metrics
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: The public baseline defines reporting discipline and common source systems, but each tenant must nominate whether IDIS, CAPER, HFA portal, lender template, project tracker, property management system, or tenant metric dictionary controls each reporting audience.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction, funder, lender, investor, and program-specific reporting sequence
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Public sources define baseline CPD, HOME, CDBG, environmental, LIHTC, and benchmark expectations, but tenants must supply the governing jurisdiction, funding stack, agreements, and authority-owner sequence before case-specific publication or submission claims are made.

notes: The source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than only one dimension.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because tenant-specific metric, jurisdiction, funding-stack, submission-authority, and source-precedence choices are represented explicitly as required runtime inputs rather than guessed.
